_protocol: Historical reconstruction: LK-99 room-temperature superconductor claim, July-August 2023

meta.title: LK-99: Room-Temperature Superconductor or Not?
meta.question: Is LK-99 (copper-substituted lead apatite) a room-temperature, ambient-pressure superconductor as claimed by Lee & Kim (2023)?
meta.resolution: Resolved when: (a) 3+ independent labs replicate zero-resistance AND diamagnetic levitation in LK-99 samples, OR (b) 3+ independent labs fail to replicate AND a credible structural/electronic explanation for the original observations emerges.

H1.label: Genuine RTSC â€” LK-99 is a room-temp superconductor
H1.description: The Lee & Kim claim is correct. LK-99 exhibits zero resistance and Meissner effect at room temperature and ambient pressure. Would be Nobel-tier discovery.

H2.label: Partial â€” real but not full SC (e.g., filamentary, diamagnetic, but not bulk SC)
H2.description: Something genuinely novel is happening in the material (unusual diamagnetism, resistivity drop) but it falls short of true superconductivity. Could be a phase transition, Cu2S impurity effect, or structural anomaly.

H3.label: Not SC â€” mundane explanation (impurities, measurement error, Cu2S)
H3.description: The observations have a conventional explanation: Cu2S phase transition at ~104C, ferromagnetism mimicking levitation, resistivity artifacts from sample geometry. No superconductivity.

H4.label: Fraud or severe methodological failure
H4.description: The original claim is based on fabricated data, cherry-picked samples, or fundamental measurement errors so severe they constitute scientific misconduct.

posteriorHistory.note: Partial levitation video + social media hype shifts H2 up slightly
DFT flat bands provide some theoretical support
Multiple failed replications + Cu2S explanation emerging
IBS single crystal definitively negative. Cu2S explains everything.
RESOLVED: Scientific consensus - not a superconductor. Cu2S impurity.

replicationTracker.label: Independent Replication Attempts
replicationTracker.description: Tracks labs attempting replication and their results. Key metric: ratio of successful to failed replications.

theoreticalSupport.label: Theoretical Plausibility
theoreticalSupport.value: DFT shows flat bands but Cu2S impurity explains all observations
theoreticalSupport.description: Do DFT/ab-initio calculations support flat bands at the Fermi level? Is the proposed mechanism (Cu substitution creating hole-doped Pb-P network) physically plausible?

tier1_critical.desc: Independent lab measures zero resistance in LK-99 sample below critical temperature
Independent lab confirms full Meissner effect (flux expulsion, not just partial levitation)
3+ independent labs fail to observe any SC signatures in high-quality samples
tier1_critical.note: 6+ labs failed replication including IBS single-crystal study

tier2_strong.desc: Lab observes partial levitation or diamagnetic response but not full Meissner
tier2_strong.note: Huazhong University video showed partial levitation (later explained by ferromagnetic Cu2S)

tier2_strong.desc: Lab observes sharp resistivity drop but not zero resistance
Cu2S structural phase transition identified as explanation for observed effects
tier2_strong.note: UMD identified Cu2S phase transition at 104C as cause of resistivity drop

tier2_strong.desc: DFT calculations show flat bands near Fermi level in Cu-substituted lead apatite
tier2_strong.note: Sinead Griffin (LBNL) DFT showed flat bands, but this alone doesn't prove SC

tier3_suggestive.desc: Viral video showing levitation/resistance drop (unvetted lab, no peer review)
tier3_suggestive.note: Multiple viral videos from Chinese labs, none showing definitive SC
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Weak signal only: H1 +2pp if corroborated

tier3_suggestive.desc: New preprint with replication attempt (positive or negative)
tier3_suggestive.note: Dozens of preprints posted, overwhelmingly negative replications

tier3_suggestive.desc: Prominent condensed matter physicists express skepticism on specific technical grounds
tier3_suggestive.note: Multiple condensed matter physicists flagged methodological issues from day one

anti_indicators.desc: Failed replication by well-resourced lab (e.g., Argonne, Max Planck)
Diamagnetic signal fully explained by ferromagnetic impurity (Cu2S or similar)
anti_indicators.note: Diamagnetic signal fully explained by ferromagnetic Cu2S impurity

anti_indicators.desc: Clean single-crystal LK-99 shows any SC signature
Original authors publish detailed methods allowing reproduction

actorModel.description: Scientific claim evaluation: who are the key actors and what drives their behavior?

originalAuthors.name: Lee Sukbae & Kim Ji-Hoon (Q-Centre, Korea University)
originalAuthors.role: Original claimants â€” published two arXiv preprints with competing author lists
originalAuthors.decisionStyle: Small lab, limited resources, long-running project (1999-2023), internal disputes visible in competing preprints
biases: Sunk cost (20+ years on this material)
Confirmation bias (interpreting ambiguous signals as SC)
filters: Peer review (bypassed by going to arXiv)
overrides: If data is fabricated, no filter catches it until replication fails

replicationLabs.name: Independent replication labs worldwide
replicationLabs.role: The actual resolution mechanism â€” their results determine truth
replicationLabs.decisionStyle: Institutional, methodical, but operating under social media time pressure
biases: Hype pressure (labs rushing synthesis, cutting corners)
filters: Materials quality (synthesis is hard, impure samples give false signals)
overrides: Well-resourced national labs (Argonne, NIMS) can produce clean samples

socialMedia.name: Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube commentary ecosystem
socialMedia.role: Amplification and narrative formation â€” not evidence producers but massive influence on attention and framing
filters: None â€” no editorial filter on social media
overrides: Expert voices can cut through but are often drowned out

methodology: REPLICATION OVER RHETORIC â€” only independent lab results move posteriors, not social media videos or takes
SAMPLE QUALITY MATTERS â€” failed replications from impure samples are weak evidence; clean single-crystal results are strong
SEPARATE THE PHENOMENA â€” partial levitation, resistivity drops, and diamagnetism are different things with different explanations
CONSIDER BASE RATES â€” room-temperature superconductor claims have a ~0% historical success rate
WATCH FOR CU2S â€” copper sulfide is a known contaminant with a phase transition at 104C that mimics SC signatures

evidenceLog.text: Two arXiv preprints posted by Lee Sukbae, Kim Ji-Hoon et al. claiming LK-99 (Pb10-xCux(PO4)6O) is a room-temperature, ambient-pressure superconductor. Papers have competing author lists suggesting internal dispute.
Social media explosion on Twitter/X and Reddit. LK-99 trending worldwide. Viral hype but no independent verification yet. Multiple unvetted videos circulating.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE - social media noise, not evidence

evidenceLog.text: Condensed matter physicists note methodological concerns: no AC susceptibility measurement, no Meissner effect demonstration, resistance measurements on polycrystalline samples are unreliable. Base rate for RT superconductor claims is effectively 0%.

Huazhong University of Science and Technology (China) posts video showing partial levitation of a dark sample on a magnet. Sample rocks but does not fully levitate - consistent with diamagnetism, not Meissner effect.
Multiple Chinese labs report synthesis attempts. Most produce non-superconducting samples. One lab (unverified) claims resistivity drop but no zero resistance.

Sinead Griffin (Lawrence Berkeley National Lab) publishes DFT calculation showing flat bands near Fermi level in Cu-substituted lead apatite structure. Theoretical plausibility for electronic correlation effects, but flat bands alone do not guarantee superconductivity.
Southeast University (Nanjing) reports failed replication: synthesized LK-99 samples show no superconducting signatures. Resistivity is semiconducting, not metallic.
IBS (Institute for Basic Science, Korea) reports synthesis of LK-99 but finds no zero resistance and no Meissner effect. Sample is a dark semiconductor.

Multiple condensed matter theory groups note that the partial levitation videos are consistent with ferromagnetism from Cu2S (copper sulfide) impurity, not diamagnetic Meissner levitation. Cu2S has a structural phase transition at ~104C.

University of Maryland team identifies Cu2S as a significant impurity phase in LK-99 synthesis. Cu2S undergoes a phase transition at 104C that produces a sharp resistivity drop - matching the original claim's 'superconducting transition'.
Prashant Jain (UIUC) et al. publish analysis showing the partial levitation is explained by ferromagnetic Cu2S impurities interacting with the permanent magnet. Not diamagnetic levitation.

IBS publishes results on high-quality single-crystal LK-99 (not polycrystalline). Single crystal shows NO superconductivity whatsoever - high resistivity semiconductor with no anomalous diamagnetism. This is the cleanest test possible.

Replication scoreboard: 0 successful replications of zero resistance, 0 Meissner effect confirmations, 6+ failed replications from independent labs worldwide. No lab has reproduced the core SC claims.

Comprehensive analysis published: ALL observed effects in LK-99 are explained by Cu2S impurity. Resistivity drop = Cu2S phase transition at 104C. Partial levitation = ferromagnetic Cu2S on permanent magnet. No superconductivity. Multiple independent groups converge on same explanation.

Nature publishes summary: LK-99 is not a superconductor. Scientific consensus reached. Original observations fully explained by Cu2S copper sulfide impurity phase. The rapid open-science replication effort is praised but the claim is definitively debunked.

watchpoints: DFT calculations from condensed matter theory groups
Any lab reporting zero resistance (not just levitation)
Cu2S impurity analysis in synthesized samples
Response from original authors to failed replications

snapshots.note: Partial levitation video + social media hype shifts H2 up slightly
DFT flat bands provide some theoretical support
Multiple failed replications + Cu2S explanation emerging
IBS single crystal definitively negative. Cu2S explains everything.
RESOLVED: Scientific consensus - not a superconductor. Cu2S impurity.

outcomes.label: Not SC â€” mundane explanation (impurities, measurement error, Cu2S)
outcomes.note: Cu2S impurity explanation confirmed by multiple independent labs. Resolved Aug 2023.

Partial levitation = ferromagnetic Cu2S on permanent magnet. No superconductivity. Multiple independent groups converge on same explanation.
unresolved.reason: Negation marker 'not' in existing entry, absent from new. Shared subjects: groups, impurity, levitation, multiple, partial

unresolved.entry_a_text: University of Maryland team identifies Cu2S as a significant impurity phase in LK-99 synthesis. Cu2S undergoes a phase transition at 104C that produces a sharp resistivity drop - matching the original claim's 'superconducting transition'.
unresolved.entry_b_text: Comprehensive analysis published: ALL observed effects in LK-99 are explained by Cu2S impurity. Resistivity drop = Cu2S phase transition at 104C. Partial levitation = ferromagnetic Cu2S on permanent magnet. No superconductivity. Multiple independent groups converge on same explanation.
unresolved.reason: Negation marker 'no' in new entry, absent from existing. Shared subjects: drop, impurity, phase, resistivity, transition

unresolved.entry_a_text: Prashant Jain (UIUC) et al. publish analysis showing the partial levitation is explained by ferromagnetic Cu2S impurities interacting with the permanent magnet. Not diamagnetic levitation.
unresolved.entry_b_text: Comprehensive analysis published: ALL observed effects in LK-99 are explained by Cu2S impurity. Resistivity drop = Cu2S phase transition at 104C. Partial levitation = ferromagnetic Cu2S on permanent magnet. No superconductivity. Multiple independent groups converge on same explanation.

unresolved.entry_a_text: IBS publishes results on high-quality single-crystal LK-99 (not polycrystalline). Single crystal shows NO superconductivity whatsoever - high resistivity semiconductor with no anomalous diamagnetism. This is the cleanest test possible.
unresolved.entry_b_text: Comprehensive analysis published: ALL observed effects in LK-99 are explained by Cu2S impurity. Resistivity drop = Cu2S phase transition at 104C. Partial levitation = ferromagnetic Cu2S on permanent magnet. No superconductivity. Multiple independent groups converge on same explanation.

unresolved.entry_a_text: University of Maryland team identifies Cu2S as a significant impurity phase in LK-99 synthesis. Cu2S undergoes a phase transition at 104C that produces a sharp resistivity drop - matching the original claim's 'superconducting transition'.
unresolved.entry_b_text: Nature publishes summary: LK-99 is not a superconductor. Scientific consensus reached. Original observations fully explained by Cu2S copper sulfide impurity phase. The rapid open-science replication effort is praised but the claim is definitively debunked.
unresolved.reason: Negation marker 'not' in new entry, absent from existing. Shared subjects: claim, impurity, original, phase
